deep_dive.title: MixCompress: Mixture of Experts for Variable Rate Learned Image Compression
deep_dive.abstract: Learned image compression (LIC) is bottlenecked by the need to store independent models for each rate-distortion operating point. Existing variable bit-rate (VBR) methods aim to reduce this overhead via dense parameter modulation, but forcing a shared backbone to approximate divergent mappings causes severe feature entanglement. Specifically, low-rate smoothing gradients inherently conflict with the preservation of high-frequency textural details, leading to sub-optimal performance. To resolve this, we propose MixCompress, a unified VBR framework based on sparse structural specialization. While sparsely gated Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) routing successfully mitigates gradient conflict, it operates on a fixed computational budget. To address the increased representational demands of higher bit-rates we introduce a Mixture-of-Depths (MoD) extension to dynamically scale model capacity. Combined with Conditional Auxiliary Transforms (CAT) for dynamic sub-band energy modulation, our hierarchical framework effectively dynamically scales capacity. Extensive evaluations demonstrate that MixCompress not only matches individually optimized single-rate baselines but can even surpass them, establishing a new Pareto frontier for computationally efficient image coding.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: MixCompress uses Mixture of Experts for variable rate image compression
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: MixCompress使用混合专家模型进行变速率图像压缩
deep_dive.llm_evidence: MixCompress使用混合专家模型进行变速率图像压缩
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: MixCompress使用混合专家模型进行变速率图像压缩
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: MixCompress proposes a sparse Mixture-of-Experts framework for variable rate learned image compression, resolving feature entanglement in dense modulation methods and achieving competitive rate-distortion performance with reduced storage
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有可变比特率图像压缩方法因强制共享主干网络导致特征纠缠，低速率平滑梯度与高频细节保留冲突。本文提出MixCompress，基于稀疏混合专家路由实现结构化专业化，避免梯度冲突。实验表明，在固定计算预算下，该方法在多个数据集上达到与独立模型相当或更优的率失真性能，并显著减少存储开销。这项研究展示了MoE在图像压缩中的有效性，并为可变比特率模型提供了新范式。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有可变比特率图像压缩方法因强制共享主干网络导致特征纠缠，低速率平滑梯度与高频细节保留冲突。本文提出MixCompress，基于稀疏混合专家路由实现结构化专业化，避免梯度冲突。实验表明，在固定计算预算下，该方法在多个数据集上达到与独立模型相当或更优的率失真性能，并显著减少存储开销。这项研究展示了MoE在图像压缩中的有效性，并为可变比特率模型提供了新范式。

quick_skim.title: Structural Composition Enables Very Fast Learning
quick_skim.abstract: AO_SCPLOWBSTRACTC_SCPLOWThere is a gap between neuroscientific theories of learning and the speed of learning observed in many experiments. Since the Cognitive Revolution of the 1950s, compositionality has played a central role in efforts to bridge this gap. Roughly, a compositional system is one where distinct modules are combined according to a set of rules in order to accomplish complex tasks. Recently, significant progress has been made in understanding the emergence of modules in both biological and artificial neural systems. How, and under what conditions, the rules of module recombination are represented in these systems remains an open question. Here we present a neural model that can leverage these rules to dramatically speed up learning. We first show that when faced with multiple tasks which share subcomponents, models learn a low-dimensional representation that captures how subcomponents are reused across the task set. These low-dimensional spaces encode the structure that governs how modules should be recombined. Restricting learning to these subspaces greatly reduces the amount of experience needed to acquire a novel task, even when learning from reinforcement on single trials. In some cases, we can leverage the geometric regularities of these representations to reduce learning to a form of hypothesis testing over a small set of discrete points. Finally, we use this theory to model both behavioral and neural data from non-human primates performing a compositional task, and show that key features in this data are consistent with a model in which exploration during learning is restricted to these low-dimensional spaces. Overall, this work shows that the advantages of modularity in neural systems can be greatly improved upon when models represent the structure of module reuse. Both these features working in tandem lead to learning on timescales similar to biological intelligences, and hence provide a model for how such fast, adaptable behavior can emerge from systems of neurons.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: module composition for fast learning, related to expert specialization in MoE
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 模块组合实现快速学习，与MoE中的专家专业化相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 模块组合实现快速学习，与MoE中的专家专业化相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 模块组合实现快速学习，与MoE中的专家专业化相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a neural model leveraging modular composition rules to accelerate learning, bridging neuroscience and artificial neural systems, conceptually aligned with expert specialization in mixture-of-experts models.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对生物和人工神经网络中学习速度慢的问题，提出一种利用模块组合规则的神经模型。通过重组预先存在的模块，该模型能显著加速学习过程。实验表明，模块化结构遵循可重组的规则，使得网络能够快速适应新任务。这项工作为理解混合专家模型中的专家分工与路由提供了理论基础，有助于设计更高效的MoE架构。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对生物和人工神经网络中学习速度慢的问题，提出一种利用模块组合规则的神经模型。通过重组预先存在的模块，该模型能显著加速学习过程。实验表明，模块化结构遵循可重组的规则，使得网络能够快速适应新任务。这项工作为理解混合专家模型中的专家分工与路由提供了理论基础，有助于设计更高效的MoE架构。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 给我查找moe中有关专家专业化有关的论文, especially work that connects or combines: expert specialization in mixture of experts; mixture of experts model; expert routing mechanism; sparse mixture of experts; conditional computation neural network; expert selection in MoE; research on expert specialization in mixture of experts models; papers about expert specialization in MoE; improving expert diversity in sparse mixture of experts.
